When the grid type and frequency changed it will report F13; 2. When the battery mode was changed to "No battery" mode,it will report F13; 3. For some old FW version, it will report F13 when the system work mode changed; 4, Generally, it will disappear automatically when shows F13; 5. If still same, and turn off the DC switch and AC switch and wait for one minute and then turn on the DC/AC switch; 6. Seek help from us, if can not go back to normal state.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Loss of data link between the Battery PDU and the Inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Loss of data link between the Battery PDU and the Inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Cable Check: Ensure the RJ45 cable is in the PCS port of the PDU and the BMS/CAN port of the inverter.
2. Pinout: Verify if your battery requires a crossover cable (some older PDU models have specific pin requirements).
3. BMS Power: Ensure the battery "Start" button was pressed to wake the BMS.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The BMS cannot read the voltage of one or more battery modules in the stack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The BMS cannot read the voltage of one or more battery modules in the stack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Internal Comms: Check the orange/black communication jumpers between each battery module in the rack.
2. Seating: Ensure every module is physically slid all the way into the rack connectors.
3. Reboot: Power down the entire rack, wait 2 mins, and restart.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The temperature sensor in one of the battery modules is providing a failed signal.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The temperature sensor in one of the battery modules is providing a failed signal.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Environment: Check if one module is near a heat source.
2. Jumpers: Inspect the small data cables connecting the modules to the PDU; a loose plug here often triggers this.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The Hall sensor or current shunt inside the PDU is malfunctioning.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The Hall sensor or current shunt inside the PDU is malfunctioning.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Hard Reset: Shutdown DC breakers and restart.
2. Firmware: This can sometimes be a software glitch; check if a BMS update is available via the USB port on the PDU.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal hardware failure of the PDU's power board.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal hardware failure of the PDU's power board.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check DC Breaker: Ensure the main battery breaker hasn't tripped due to an external short.
2. Replacement: If the error returns after a full restart, the PDU (Control Box) likely needs replacement.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The number of modules detected doesn't match the system setting.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The number of modules detected doesn't match the system setting.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Count Modules: If you have 10 modules, ensure the BMS is set to recognize 10.
2. DIP Switches: Verify the DIP switch settings on the PDU match the total capacity of the stack.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: General alarm often caused by Cell Imbalance or Over/Under Voltage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: General alarm often caused by Cell Imbalance or Over/Under Voltage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check SOC: If the battery is at 0%, charge it immediately from the grid.
2. Top Balance: If the battery is near 100%, leave it connected for 48 hours to allow the BMS to balance the cells.
3. Check LCD: Look for specific "Cell High Volt" or "Cell Low Volt" sub-messages.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The Inverter Power Module is drawing too much current or is overheating.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The Inverter Power Module is drawing too much current or is overheating.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Airflow: Ensure the outdoor unit coils aren't clogged with dust.
2. Restart: Power off for 5 minutes. If it returns immediately, the IPM board may be shorted.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: AC or DC input voltage is outside the allowed range (too high or too low).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: AC or DC input voltage is outside the allowed range (too high or too low).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Measure DC: Check if the solar panel voltage (Voc) exceeds 380V.
2. Measure AC: Ensure your grid voltage is within 208V–240V.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The compressor is overheating.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The compressor is overheating.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Refrigerant Check: Usually caused by a gas leak (low pressure).
2. Blockage: Check if the expansion valve is clogged.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The controller lost synchronization with the compressor motor.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The controller lost synchronization with the compressor motor.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Cables: Ensure the U-V-W wires to the compressor are tight.
2. Internal Board: Could be a faulty main outdoor PCB.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Solar panel input is too high during peak sun.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Solar panel input is too high during peak sun.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. String Count: Ensure you haven't wired too many panels in series (Max Voc is typically 380V).
2. Unload: If the AC stops suddenly, the DC voltage "spikes"—ensure the AC is properly grounded.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The indoor and outdoor units cannot "talk" to each other.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The indoor and outdoor units cannot "talk" to each other.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Signal Wire: Check the communication wire (usually wire #3 or S) between units.
2. Grounding: Ensure the signal wire is shielded or kept away from power lines to avoid interference.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The room temperature sensor is broken or disconnected.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The room temperature sensor is broken or disconnected.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Plug Check: Open the indoor panel and re-seat the black-tipped sensor plug.
2. Resistance Test: The sensor should read roughly 10kΩ–20kΩ at room temp.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The evaporator coil sensor has failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The evaporator coil sensor has failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Inspect Copper: Check if the sensor has slipped out of its copper holder on the coil.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The sensor on the back of the outdoor unit is faulty.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The sensor on the back of the outdoor unit is faulty.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Visual Check: Look for wires bitten by rodents or damaged by UV/weather.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The condenser coil sensor has failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The condenser coil sensor has failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Replace Sensor: These are high-exposure parts; they may need replacement if the unit is near the ocean (corrosion).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The sensor on the compressor discharge pipe is faulty.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The sensor on the compressor discharge pipe is faulty.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Heat Check: Ensure the sensor hasn't melted due to extreme compressor heat.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The indoor blower fan is stuck or the motor is dead.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The indoor blower fan is stuck or the motor is dead.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Manual Spin: With power off, try to spin the blower with your hand. If it doesn't spin freely, the bearings are gone.
2. Capacitor/PCB: If it spins freely, the fan motor or the indoor PCB is faulty.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The outdoor fan is blocked or the motor has failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The outdoor fan is blocked or the motor has failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Obstruction: Check for sticks, leaves, or bird nests inside the outdoor unit.
2. Wiring: Check the 3-pin or 5-pin plug from the fan to the outdoor PCB.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The indoor unit's memory chip has failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The indoor unit's memory chip has failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Power Cycle: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes.
2. Replace PCB: If it returns, the indoor mainboard must be replaced.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Outdoor fan speed is outside the normal range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Outdoor fan speed is outside the normal range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Wind Interference: High winds blowing directly into the fan can cause this.
2. Motor Failure: Check if the fan motor is overheating.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The logger is not receiving power from the inverter's USB/RS485 port.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The logger is not receiving power from the inverter's USB/RS485 port.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Connection: Ensure the stick is fully screwed into the port.
2. Port Check: Inspect the inverter port for bent pins or debris.
3. Inverter Status: The inverter must be "On" (PV or AC power) for the port to be active.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The logger is not connected to the local WiFi router or 4G tower.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The logger is not connected to the local WiFi router or 4G tower.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Signal Strength: Move the router closer or use a WiFi extender.
2. Credentials: Re-run the setup in the app to ensure the WiFi password is correct.
3. Frequency: Ensure you are using 2.4GHz WiFi (5GHz is not supported).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The logger is connected to the internet but cannot "talk" to the inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The logger is connected to the internet but cannot "talk" to the inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Address Check: Ensure the Inverter Modbus ID is set to "1" (default).
2. Firmware: The inverter firmware may be too old to support that specific logger model.
3. Reseat: Unplug the logger, wait 30 seconds, and plug it back in.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Connected to WiFi, but cannot reach the Deye/Solarman cloud server.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Connected to WiFi, but cannot reach the Deye/Solarman cloud server.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. DNS/Firewall: Check if your router is blocking ports 10000 or 10001.
2. Internet: Ensure the local internet is actually active (check on a phone).
3. Server Maintenance: Rarely, the Solarman server may be down.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The logger has lost power or internet connection for >10 mins.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The logger has lost power or internet connection for >10 mins.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Power Cycle: Unplug the stick and plug it back in.
2. Check Router: If the router restarted, the stick may need a few minutes to reconnect.
3. 4G Credit: If using a 4G stick, ensure the SIM card has active data.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Intermittent WiFi signal or server delay.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Intermittent WiFi signal or server delay.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. WiFi Channel: Change your router's WiFi channel to a less congested one (Channel 1, 6, or 11).
2. Signal Wall: If there is a thick concrete wall between the logger and router, use a WiFi repeater.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The Logger Serial Number is already registered to another account.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The Logger Serial Number is already registered to another account.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Unbind: If you bought the unit used, the previous owner must "Unbind" it from their app.
2. Manual Entry: Double-check that you didn't mistype the SN (Serial Number) or use the Inverter SN by mistake.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Configuration timeout during the "SmartConfig" process.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Configuration timeout during the "SmartConfig" process.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Manual Mode: Switch the logger to AP Mode (the logger will broadcast its own WiFi signal like AP_XXXXXX). Connect your phone to that signal and configure via 10.10.10.1 in your browser.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: One or more cells have exceeded the safety voltage limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: One or more cells have exceeded the safety voltage limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Balance: Leave the system at 100% SOC for 24 hours to allow the BMS to top-balance cells.
2. Inverter Settings: Check if the "Bulk Charge" voltage on the inverter is set too high for the module count.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery is critically depleted (usually <5% SOC).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Battery is critically depleted (usually <5% SOC).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Manual Charge: Force a charge from the Grid or Generator immediately.
2. Hibernation: If it won't start, the BMS may be in "Deep Sleep." Press the "Start" button on the PDU for 5+ seconds.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal module temperature is above 55°C.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal module temperature is above 55°C.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Fans: Verify the PDU fans are spinning.
2. Environment: Ensure the battery rack isn't in a small, unventilated room or near a boiler/inverter exhaust.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: A leakage current is detected between the high-voltage DC bus and the metal rack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: A leakage current is detected between the high-voltage DC bus and the metal rack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Cabling: Check the orange (+) and black (-) power jumpers between modules for any nicks.
2. Moisture: Ensure no condensation is present inside the PDU or on the module terminals.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The charge or discharge current exceeds the system's limit (e.g., >160A).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The charge or discharge current exceeds the system's limit (e.g., >160A).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Load Check: Reduce the heavy loads being pulled from the inverter.
2. Short Circuit: Inspect the DC cables between the PDU and the inverter for short circuits.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The inverter has lost the data link to the smart meter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The inverter has lost the data link to the smart meter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Wiring: Ensure the RS485 A/B wires aren't swapped.
2. Check Port: Ensure the cable is in the "Meter" or "RS485" port, not the BMS port.
3. Address: Ensure the Meter Modbus ID is set correctly (usually 001).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The meter detects that the phase sequence (L1, L2, L3) is incorrect.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The meter detects that the phase sequence (L1, L2, L3) is incorrect.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Rotation: Swap any two phase wires at the meter input to correct the rotation.
2. CT Orientation: Ensure the CT clamps are pointing in the same direction (K \rightarrow L) toward the grid.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Incorrect password entered while trying to change settings.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Incorrect password entered while trying to change settings.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Default Pass: Try the default password (usually 1000 or 0000).
2. Lockout: If it persists, power cycle the meter by flipping the AC breaker.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The current or voltage being measured exceeds the meter's capacity.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The current or voltage being measured exceeds the meter's capacity.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. CT Ratio: If using a CT-type meter, ensure the CT ratio is set correctly in the meter's menu.
2. Current Check: Verify the actual load doesn't exceed the meter's 60A/80A limit (for direct-connect).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The meter is not sensing voltage on one or more phases.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The meter is not sensing voltage on one or more phases.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Fuses: If the meter uses external voltage fuses, check if one has blown.
2. Bridge Wires: Ensure the voltage sensing bridges (small wires) are tight.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The meter shows negative wattage while consuming power.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The meter shows negative wattage while consuming power.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. CT Direction: Flip the CT clamps on the main lines. The arrow must point from the Grid to the House.
2. A/B Swap: Check if the RS485 wires are crossed.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Power / Relay Open
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Power / Relay Open
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check AC Plug: Ensure the relay box is firmly plugged into the household socket.
2. Check Inverter: Ensure the microinverter is connected and receiving DC power from the panels.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Interconnection Active
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Interconnection Active
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: This is normal. It indicates the relay is closed and power is flowing to the grid.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Communication
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Communication
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Distance: Ensure the relay box is within 5 meters of the microinverter.
2. Re-pair: Press and hold the SET button for 10 seconds to force a reconnection.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: System Self-Test
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: System Self-Test
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Normal during startup. If it persists for >2 minutes, the internal relay has failed its safety check.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Internal hardware failure or "Stuck" relay contact.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal hardware failure or "Stuck" relay contact.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal hardware failure or "Stuck" relay contact.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Power Cycle: Unplug from AC, wait 2 minutes, and plug back in.
2. Factory Reset: Hold the SET button for 15+ seconds until all lights flash.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Grid voltage or frequency is outside the relay's safety window.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid voltage or frequency is outside the relay's safety window.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid voltage or frequency is outside the relay's safety window.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Safety Profile: Check the "Grid Standard" setting in the web interface (e.g., VDE-AR-N 4105).
2. Grid Stability: Measure your household AC voltage; if it's >253V, the relay will stay open for safety.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: The relay box cannot reach the monitoring cloud.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The relay box cannot reach the monitoring cloud.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The relay box cannot reach the monitoring cloud.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Router: Ensure the WiFi router is connected to the internet.
2. Firewall: Ensure ports 10000 and 10001 are open on your local network.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Port: Ensure the stick is fully threaded into the inverter's RS485/USB port.
2. Inverter Power: The inverter must have DC (PV) or AC power to energize the port.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Gateway Connection
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Gateway Connection
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Range: Ensure the LoRa Gateway is within 500m–1km (depending on obstructions).
2. Antenna: Check that the LoRa antenna is screwed on tight and pointing vertically.
3. Sync: Re-pair the TX stick with the Gateway using the "ID Search" function in the Gateway menu.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Inverter Data
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Inverter Data
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Modbus ID: Ensure the Inverter's Modbus address is set to 01.
2. Protocol: Verify the inverter is set to "Solarman" or "Deye" protocol in the settings.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Firmware Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Firmware Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Reset: Unplug the stick, wait 30 seconds, and plug it back in.
2. Update: Check the app for a "Remote Upgrade" notification.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: The TX stick has lost power or moved out of range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The TX stick has lost power or moved out of range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The TX stick has lost power or moved out of range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Physical Obstacles: Check if new metal structures (roofing, containers) were placed between the TX and Gateway.
2. Interference: Move the LoRa Gateway away from heavy WiFi routers or high-voltage transformers.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: The Serial Number (SN) of the TX stick is not registered to the Gateway.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The Serial Number (SN) of the TX stick is not registered to the Gateway.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The Serial Number (SN) of the TX stick is not registered to the Gateway.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Whitelisting: In the Gateway's web interface, manually add the TX stick's SN to the "Authorized Nodes" list.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Weak RF signal.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Weak RF signal.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Antenna Height: Raise the Gateway antenna as high as possible.
2. Cable: If using an antenna extension cable, ensure it is low-loss (LMR-400) and under 3 meters.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: The TX stick is connected but the inverter isn't sending packets.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The TX stick is connected but the inverter isn't sending packets.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The TX stick is connected but the inverter isn't sending packets.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Baud Rate: Ensure the inverter's RS485 baud rate is set to 9600 (default).
2. Port Conflict: Ensure no other logger (WiFi/GPRS) is plugged into a parallel port on the same inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The inverter commanded the switch to close, but the internal "Auxiliary Contact" didn't confirm.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The inverter commanded the switch to close, but the internal "Auxiliary Contact" didn't confirm.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Control Wiring: Check the 12V or 230V control signal wires between the inverter and the switch.
2. Manual Test: If the switch has a manual toggle, check if it is physically jammed.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Control Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Control Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Input: Verify that the 230V AC or 12V DC control power is reaching the supply terminals.
2. Internal Fuse: Some models have a small internal glass fuse; check if it has blown.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Communication Loss
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Communication Loss
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Signal Cable: Check the RS485 (A/B) wiring to the inverter.
2. Address: Ensure the Smart Switch Modbus ID (DIP switches) matches the inverter's expected ID.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Waiting for Grid/Sync
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Waiting for Grid/Sync
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Normal State: The switch is waiting for the grid voltage to stabilize before closing.
2. Timer: Check the "Grid Reconnect Time" in the inverter settings (usually 60–120 seconds).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Hardware Contact Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Hardware Contact Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Internal Relay: The internal contactor may be welded shut or physically broken.
2. Hard Reset: Power down the entire system for 10 minutes and restart.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Normal
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Normal
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: The optimizer is communicating and tracking MPPT.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Offline
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Offline
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
Lost communication with the Gateway/Inverter.
1. Check DC: Ensure the sun is out; the unit needs PV power to "talk."
2. Range: If using a LoRa/WiFi bridge, check the signal strength.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Fault / Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Fault / Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
A specific electrical fault has occurred.
1. Over-Voltage: Check if the panel V_{oc} exceeds 80V.
2. Over-Temp: Ensure at least 2cm of space between the optimizer and the panel for airflow.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Interrupted
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Interrupted
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
Communication is unstable.
1. Wiring: Check for loose MC4 connectors in the string.
2. Interference: Move high-power AC cables away from the DC strings.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Module or Optimizer Failure
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Module or Optimizer Failure
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ammeter Test: Use a clip-on ammeter on the input side. If 0A, the solar panel is likely damaged or heavily shaded.
2. Bypass Test: Disconnect the optimizer and connect the panel directly to the string. If the string starts working, the optimizer is faulty.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Leakage to Ground
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Leakage to Ground
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Cable Check: Look for "pinched" cables where the optimizer is mounted to the aluminum rail.
2. Moisture: Check if MC4 connectors are sitting in a puddle on a flat roof.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Open Circuit
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Open Circuit
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Series Link: Since optimizers are in series, one bad connection breaks the whole chain. Check each "OUT" connection in the string.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Breaker: Ensure the dedicated EV breaker in your DB board is ON.
2. Wiring: Verify the L, N, and PE wires are securely tightened in the terminal block.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Hardware / Safety Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Hardware / Safety Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Emergency Stop: Check if the Red E-Stop button on the side is pressed. Twist to release.
2. Over-Temp: The unit may be too hot. Ensure it's not in direct sunlight.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Electrical Input Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Electrical Input Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Voltage: Grid voltage is too high (>265V) or too low (<180V).
2. Frequency: Grid frequency is unstable. Often happens when running on a low-quality generator.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Communication Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Communication Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. CP Signal: The "Control Pilot" signal to the car is weak. Ensure the plug is fully pushed into the car's socket.
2. Vehicle Ready: The car may be scheduled to charge later or its battery is already full.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Improper or missing Earth (PE) connection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Improper or missing Earth (PE) connection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Test Earth: Use a multimeter to check the resistance between Neutral and Earth; it should be very low (typically <5Ω).
2. PE Wire: Ensure the green/yellow wire is connected to a dedicated ground rod.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: The built-in Type B RCD detected a DC or AC leak.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The built-in Type B RCD detected a DC or AC leak.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The built-in Type B RCD detected a DC or AC leak.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Moisture: Check the charging gun and the car's port for water or debris.
2. Cable Damage: Inspect the thick charging cable for cuts or heavy abrasions.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Vehicle communication handshake failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Vehicle communication handshake failed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Reseat Plug: Unplug from the car and wait 10 seconds before plugging back in.
2. Dirty Pins: Use a dry electronic cleaner to clean the small data pins inside the connector.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: The internal high-power contactor is stuck.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The internal high-power contactor is stuck.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The internal high-power contactor is stuck.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Hard Reset: Turn the breaker OFF for 5 minutes, then back ON. If the error remains, the internal relay needs professional replacement.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check DC String: The concentrator is powered via the PV string loop or a separate 24V adapter. Ensure the input voltage is within the 12V–24V range.
2. Wiring: Verify the polarity of the power input terminals.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: System Initialization Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: System Initialization Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Memory Check: The internal storage may have failed. Restart the unit by cycling power.
2. Config Mismatch: If you just added new optimizers, the system may be stuck in a "Search" loop. Wait 10 minutes.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: PLC Comm Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PLC Comm Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Signal Noise: High-frequency noise from the inverter is interfering with the Power Line Communication. Ensure the PV cables are separated from AC cables by at least 10cm.
2. Bridge: If the string is too long, the signal may be too weak.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: WiFi/Cloud Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: WiFi/Cloud Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Signal Strength: Check the distance to the WiFi router.
2. Whitelisting: Ensure the router isn't blocking the concentrator’s MAC address.
3. Manual Config: Log into 10.10.10.1 and re-enter the WiFi credentials.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Concentrator has lost power or internet.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Concentrator has lost power or internet.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Concentrator has lost power or internet.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Router: Verify if other devices on the site have internet.
2. Re-Pairing: If the router password changed, you must reset the concentrator and re-pair.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: The concentrator cannot find the registered optimizers.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The concentrator cannot find the registered optimizers.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The concentrator cannot find the registered optimizers.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. QR Code Check: Ensure the Optimizer SNs were scanned into the correct string in the app layout.
2. String Continuity: Use a multimeter to ensure the DC string loop is closed.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: The safety "Stop" signal has been triggered.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The safety "Stop" signal has been triggered.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The safety "Stop" signal has been triggered.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check E-Stop: Verify if an external emergency switch is pressed.
2. Inverter Fault: If the inverter is off, the concentrator will trigger RSD for safety.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Two optimizers have been assigned the same SN.
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Two optimizers have been assigned the same SN.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Two optimizers have been assigned the same SN.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Layout Review: Check your physical layout map. Delete the duplicate SN in the app and re-scan.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Not Connected
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Not Connected
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
The CT unit is not communicating with the inverter.
1. Scan Channel: Go to Param Setting \rightarrow Scan Chan to find the inverter's LoRa frequency.
2. Distance: Ensure the CT is within 200m (line-of-sight) or 20-50m through walls.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Wireless Linked
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Wireless Linked
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: The unit is connected via LoRa. If data is still 0, check physical CT clamps.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Wired Linked
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Wired Linked
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: The unit is connected via RS485. Ensure the LoRa antenna isn't conflicting with wired settings.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Scan Failed
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Scan Failed
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Antenna: Ensure the external LoRa antenna is screwed tightly to the CT box.
2. Inverter Setup: Ensure "Wireless CT" is enabled in the inverter's Advanced Function menu.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: CT Clamps are Reversed
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: CT Clamps are Reversed
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Direction: Check the arrow on the CT clamp. It must point toward the inverter/load (away from the grid).
2. Phase Match: Ensure the CT on L1 is monitoring the same wire connected to the L1 voltage input of the CT unit.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: CT Not Plugged In
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: CT Not Plugged In
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Socket: Check the 3.5mm jack or screw terminals on the bottom of the CT unit.
2. CT Ratio: Ensure the "CT Ratio" in Param Setting matches your physical CTs (Default is usually 2000:1 or 100A/50mA).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Phase Sequence Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Phase Sequence Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Wiring: For 3-phase systems, ensure L1, L2, L3, and Neutral are in the correct order. The unit powers itself from the L1/N voltage.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal communication failure inside the inverter control boards/DSP communication path.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal communication error or control board communication abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Turn off the DC switch, wait until the display/LCD is off, turn the DC switch on again and verify operation. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Memory chip read/write failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: EEPROM/memory chip abnormality or internal control board failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power-cycle the inverter with the DC switch. If the fault persists after restart, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid frequency is outside the inverter/grid-standard operating range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid frequency abnormality, unstable grid, or incorrect grid/safety setting.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: The inverter may restart automatically when grid returns to normal. Check grid frequency and grid connection cables. Confirm safety country/grid standard. Contact service if unresolved.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Relay self-checking failure during inverter startup/self-test.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal relay check abnormality or relay/control circuit issue.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Turn off the DC switch, wait until the display is off, restart the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery voltage is lower than the discharge limit (<40V for LV, or below the HV string limit).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Battery voltage is lower than the discharge limit (<40V for LV, or below the HV string limit).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check SOC: If the battery is at 0%, the BMS shuts down.
2. Force Charge: Enable "Charge from Grid" in the SEMS+ app to wake the battery.
3. Manual Start: Press the "Power/Start" button on the battery module for 5 seconds.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery voltage is higher than the charging limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Battery voltage is higher than the charging limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Settings: Ensure the battery brand/model selected in the SolarGo app matches the hardware.
2. BMS Link: If the inverter can't see the BMS, it may misread the voltage.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Communication error between LCD DSP and Master DSP.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Display/control communication abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power-cycle the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC component injected into the AC output exceeds inverter limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal inverter output/control abnormality or grid/output current issue.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Turn off the DC switch, wait until the display is off, restart the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Insulation resistance between PV array and earth/ground is too low.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PV string insulation issue, damaged DC cable/connector, moisture ingress, or grounding problem.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check impedance between PV (+)/PV (-) and earth. Ensure grounding is correct. If impedance is below the manual threshold, inspect PV string wiring and insulation. Contact service if the value is normal but the fault persists.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid voltage is outside the inverter/grid-standard operating range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid voltage abnormality, loose AC wiring, neutral/PE wiring issue, or incorrect grid standard.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: The inverter may restart automatically when grid returns to normal. Check grid voltage, AC terminals, neutral (N), PE and grid standard settings. Contact service if unresolved.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: External fan failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: External cooling fan blocked, disconnected, damaged, or fan drive abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Inspect external fan for blockage/dust, check fan connection, restart inverter. If the fan does not recover, contact service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: PV array/DC input voltage exceeds or is too close to inverter maximum input voltage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PV string design voltage too high, cold-weather open-circuit voltage too high, or wrong string configuration.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check PV open-circuit voltage against inverter maximum input voltage. Correct string configuration if needed. Contact service if voltage is within specification but fault persists.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Inverter internal/case temperature is higher than normal.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: High ambient temperature, poor ventilation, blocked heat sink/airflow, or installation location too hot.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Reduce ambient temperature, improve ventilation, clean heat sink/air intake/outlet, move inverter to a cooler place if needed. Contact service if unresolved.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal fan failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal cooling fan blocked, disconnected, damaged, or fan drive abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Inspect/clean fan path where accessible, restart inverter. If the fault remains, contact service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal DC bus voltage is too high.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal DC bus/control circuit abnormality or DC side transient/overvoltage condition.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Turn off the DC switch, wait until the display is off, restart the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Ground leakage/residual current is too high.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Excessive leakage current from PV/AC side, grounding issue, insulation problem, or external AC system issue.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Unplug PV inputs and check the peripheral AC system. After the problem is cleared, reconnect PV panels and check inverter status. Contact service if unresolved.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid is disconnected or unavailable.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No grid connection, AC breaker open, loose AC cable, grid outage, or grid-side fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check whether grid is connected, inspect grid connection cables/breaker, and confirm grid availability. The inverter should recover after grid returns to normal.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Output/AC current sensor failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: AC current sensor abnormality or internal measurement circuit failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power-cycle the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Relay self-checking failure during inverter startup/self-test.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal relay check abnormality or relay/control circuit issue.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Turn off the DC switch, wait until the display is off, restart the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Leakage current detection circuit failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal GFCI/leakage current detection circuit abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power-cycle the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The inverter is set to "Lithium Battery" mode but detects no battery voltage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The inverter is set to "Lithium Battery" mode but detects no battery voltage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Breaker: Ensure the DC battery breaker/switch is turned ON.
2. Pre-charge: If the voltage is too low, the inverter won't "see" it. Check battery terminals with a multimeter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: 1.5V reference voltage exceeds/fails the expected limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal reference voltage/control circuit abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power-cycle the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: AC/output current sensor failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: AC current sensor abnormality or internal measurement circuit failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power-cycle the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Leakage current detection circuit failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal GFCI/leakage current detection circuit abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power-cycle the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Too much DC current is leaking into the AC output.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Too much DC current is leaking into the AC output.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Restart: Usually a transient logic error. Restart the system.
2. Hardware: If it persists, the internal output relay or DC-filter may be damaged.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Arc-fault detection/AFCI fault on DC/PV side.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Loose or wrong DC connector, damaged PV cable, poor connection, or actual DC arc condition.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Turn off DC switch, remove/check DC connector, confirm connector model and proper connection, then restart. If the fault persists, inspect PV cabling and contact service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Auxiliary/air fan fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Fan blocked, disconnected, damaged or fan drive abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Inspect fan for blockage/dust, restart inverter. If the fault remains, contact service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal DC bus voltage is unbalanced.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal DC bus/control circuit abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power-cycle the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: N-PE Bond Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: N-PE Bond Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Wiring: Many regions require the ABD to create a Neutral-to-Earth bond during backup. Ensure the internal relay for the N-PE link is configured correctly in the app.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Standby
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Standby
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Normal. The battery is waiting for a command from the inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Router Not Found
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Router Not Found
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Signal: The inverter is too far from the WiFi router.
2. Credentials: Use the SolarGo app to re-enter the WiFi password.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Idle
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Idle
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Normal. The battery is fully charged and sitting ready.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Server Problem
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Server Problem
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Internet: The router has no internet access.
2. Firewall: Ensure the network allows outgoing traffic on ports 10000 and 20000.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: System Resetting
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: System Resetting
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Reload: If the "Reload" button was pressed for 10s, it is performing a factory reset. Wait 2 minutes.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Scheduled / Waiting
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Scheduled / Waiting
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: The charger is connected, but waiting for a preset time (off-peak) or a command from the app.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Charging
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Charging
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Normal operation. Energy is flowing to the vehicle.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Inverter Comm Fail
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Inverter Comm Fail
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. RS485 Chain: Check the daisy-chain wiring from the SEC1000 to the first inverter.
2. Terminator: Ensure the 120Ω termination resistor is ON at the last inverter in the chain.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No RS485 Link
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No RS485 Link
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Wiring: Check the RS485 A/B pins. On the inverter side, it’s usually the "Meter" port.
2. Voltage: The meter must be powered by AC (L1/N) to communicate.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: One or more cells in the stack are above the safe limit (>3.65V).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: One or more cells in the stack are above the safe limit (>3.65V).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Balancing: Keep the battery connected at 100% SOC for 24 hours to allow passive balancing.
2. Inverter Logic: Ensure the inverter's "Max Charge Voltage" hasn't been manually set too high.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Battery Systems (Lynx Home U & F Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery has discharged too deep (<2.5V per cell).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Battery has discharged too deep (<2.5V per cell).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Immediate Charge: Use the "Force Charge" function on the inverter.
2. Sleep Mode: If the LED is off, press the "Start" button for 5 seconds to wake the BMS.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Battery Systems (Lynx Home U & F Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal module temperature is >55°C.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal module temperature is >55°C.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Shade: Ensure the battery is not in a sunroom or near the inverter's hot exhaust.
2. Cool Down: Shut down the system and let it rest for 2 hours.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Battery Systems (Lynx Home U & F Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal module temperature is <0°C.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal module temperature is <0°C.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Charging Block: LFP batteries cannot be charged in freezing temps. The BMS will stop charging until the temp reaches 5°C.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Battery Systems (Lynx Home U & F Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Charging current is higher than the battery's BMS limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Charging current is higher than the battery's BMS limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Inverter Limit: In the SolarGo app, lower the "Max Charge Current" (e.g., from 50A to 25A).
2. Parallel Link: Ensure all battery modules are correctly connected; if one module is disconnected, the others take too much current.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Battery Systems (Lynx Home U & F Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The Master module cannot "talk" to the Slave modules in the stack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The Master module cannot "talk" to the Slave modules in the stack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Comms Cable: Check the short RJ45 cables between battery modules.
2. DIP Switches: Ensure the Master is set to "ID 1" and Slaves are addressed sequentially (or as per manual).
vendor_mappings.device_family: Battery Systems (Lynx Home U & F Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Leakage current detected between the battery bus and the casing.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Leakage current detected between the battery bus and the casing.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Power Plugs: Check the high-voltage orange/black plugs for moisture or dust.
2. Grounding: Ensure the battery rack is earthed to the same point as the inverter.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Battery Systems (Lynx Home U & F Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The battery failed to pre-charge the internal capacitors before closing the relay.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The battery failed to pre-charge the internal capacitors before closing the relay.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Inverter Bus: Turn the Inverter ON before turning the battery on.
2. Check DC Fuse: An internal fuse in the battery PDU may have blown.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Battery Systems (Lynx Home U & F Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Upgrading
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Upgrading
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: DO NOT power off. The system is updating firmware.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Standby
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Standby
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Normal. The charger is ready and waiting for a vehicle connection.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Loose mounting or high load.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Loose mounting or high load.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Tighten Bolts: Ensure the transformer is securely bolted to the wall/bracket.
2. Saturation: Check if the backup loads exceed the transformer's KVA rating.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: CT Placement Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: CT Placement Error
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Direction: Ensure the arrow on the CT clamp points toward the Loads/House (away from the Grid).
2. Phase Match: CT1 must be on the same wire as the L1 voltage reference. If you swap them, the math fails.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: RSD Engaged
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: RSD Engaged
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Handshake Fail: If the inverter is ON but volts are still low, the PLC (Power Line Communication) signal is being blocked. Keep DC cables away from AC noise.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Control Pilot communication failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Control Pilot communication failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Reseat: Unplug and firmly re-insert the Type 2 connector.
2. Pins: Check for bent pins or debris inside the charging gun.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: (For 3-phase units) Missing phase or incorrect rotation.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: (For 3-phase units) Missing phase or incorrect rotation.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Wiring: Ensure L1, L2, and L3 are all present and in the correct order at the input terminal.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal contactor failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal contactor failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Hard Reset: Power off the breaker for 5 minutes. If it remains after restart, the internal hardware is faulty.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Leakage current detected (>6mA DC or >30mA AC).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Leakage current detected (>6mA DC or >30mA AC).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Inspect Cable: Check the charging gun and cable for cuts or moisture.
2. Car Side: Try plugging into a different EV to rule out a fault in the vehicle's onboard charger.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Network Fail
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Network Fail
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. LAN Cable: Check the Ethernet connection to the router.
2. DNS: Ensure the router is providing an IP (DHCP) or set a Static IP via the configuration tool.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Reversed CTs
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Reversed CTs
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Flip Clamp: If you see "Export" when you are clearly "Importing," physically flip the CT clamp on the main wire.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Input Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Input Power
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. PV Connection: Ensure the panel MC4 connectors are firmly snapped into the "Input" side of the RSD module.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal Fuse / Breaker trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal Fuse / Breaker trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Thermal Switch: Many units have a self-resetting thermal switch. Let the unit cool for 30 minutes.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The inverter display is off / no display shown.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Insufficient PV voltage, DC connector issue, wrong module configuration, or internal/display issue.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Turn off DC switch and unplug DC connector, measure PV array voltage. Reconnect DC connector and turn on DC switch. If PV voltage is below startup threshold, check module configuration; if voltage is sufficient, contact service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DNS Series / single-phase PV inverter; GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal device failure shown without a specific numeric code.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal inverter hardware/control failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Restart inverter once. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service with serial number, software version, installation date, fault time and fault screenshots.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe DT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Unbalanced phase loading.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Unbalanced phase loading.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Check Loads: If one 120V leg has 4kW and the other has 100W, the transformer works overtime. Re-distribute 120V breakers in the panel.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Input / External Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Input / External Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Grid Voltage: Voltage is outside the 180V–265V range.
2. Grounding: Missing or poor Earth connection detected. Check the PE terminal.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: System Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: System Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
Immediate Action:
1. Check if the Emergency Stop button is pressed. Twist to release.
2. Check for Over-Temperature. If the unit is in direct sun, let it cool.
vendor_mappings.device_family: E-Mobility / EV Chargers (HCA & HCA G2 Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: General BMS hardware failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: General BMS hardware failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Hard Reset: Turn off the battery switch, wait 3 minutes, and turn it back on.
2. Firmware: Check for a BMS firmware update in the SolarGo app.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Battery Systems (Lynx Home U & F Series)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid Instability
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid Instability
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Chattering: The grid voltage is hovering right at the limit. Increase the "Wait Time" in the inverter settings to prevent constant switching.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Serial/internal communication failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal communication/control circuit abnormality.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power-cycle the inverter. If the fault persists, contact GoodWe/local service.
vendor_mappings.device_family: GoodWe MT Series / three-phase PV inverter; GoodWe XS Series / single-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Normal
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Normal
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: The unit is successfully connected to the SEMS Portal.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Active Shutdown
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Active Shutdown
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Trigger Check: Verify if the AC power to the transmitter/inverter is OFF.
2. E-Stop: Check if the physical emergency button has been pressed.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: PDU Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PDU Fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Isolation: Check the grounding of the metal battery cabinet.
2. Reset: Turn off the bottom DC switch on the stack, wait 2 mins, and restart.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Synchronization
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Synchronization
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Standard Delay: The ABD must wait for the inverter to match the phase/frequency of the internal "Island." This can take 10–60 seconds.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal contactor is stuck.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal contactor is stuck.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Unplug the car.
2. Power cycle the Wallbox breaker. If it doesn't clear, it's a hardware failure.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The car's onboard charger has a small DC leak (>6mA).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The car's onboard charger has a small DC leak (>6mA).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Inspect the charging cable for damage.
2. Try charging a different EV to see if the error is with the car or the box.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid voltage is <150V or a phase is missing.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid voltage is <150V or a phase is missing.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check the AC supply into the Wallbox.
2. Ensure the Neutral wire is securely connected (floating neutrals cause this).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: High String Input Voltage / High string input voltage
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: High String Input Voltage / High string input voltage. The PV array is incorrectly configured. Excessive PV modules are connected in series to the PV string, and therefore the open-circuit voltage exceeds the maximum inverter input voltage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The PV array is incorrectly configured. Excessive PV modules are connected in series to the PV string, and therefore the open-circuit voltage exceeds the maximum inverter input voltage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Reduce the number of PV modules connected in series to the PV string until the PV string open-circuit voltage is less than or equal to the maximum inverter input voltage. After the PV array is correctly configured, the inverter alarm disappears. The following is the mapping between PV strings and alarm cause IDs: • Cause ID 1: string 1 • Cause ID 2: string 2
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: DC Arc Fault / DC arc fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC Arc Fault / DC arc fault. The PV string power cable arcs or is in poor contact. Cause ID 1 = PV1 and PV2 Cause ID 2 = PV3 and PV4
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The PV string power cable arcs or is in poor contact. Cause ID 1 = PV1 and PV2 Cause ID 2 = PV3 and PV4
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check that the PV string power cable does not arc and is in good contact.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC arc fault. The PV string power cables arc or are in poor contact. ● Cause ID 1 = PV1 ● Cause ID 2 = PV2
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The PV string power cables arc or are in poor contact. ● Cause ID 1 = PV1 ● Cause ID 2 = PV2
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check whether the PV string cables arc or are in poor contact.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: String Reversed / String reverse connectio n / String Reverse Connection
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: String Reversed / String reverse connectio n / String Reverse Connection. The PV string is reversely connected.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The PV string is reversely connected.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check whether the PV string is reversely connected to the SUN2000L. If so, wait until the solar irradiance declines at night and the PV string current reduces to below 0.5 A. Then, turn off the DC switch and correct the PV string polarity. The following is the mapping between PV strings and alarm cause IDs: • Cause ID 1: string 1 • Cause ID 2: string 2
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: String current backfeed / String Current Backfeed
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: String current backfeed / String Current Backfeed. The number of PV modules connected in series to this PV string is insufficient. As a result, the end voltage is lower than that of other strings. Cause ID 1 = PV1 Cause ID 2 = PV2 Cause ID 3 = PV3 Cause ID 4 = PV4
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The number of PV modules connected in series to this PV string is insufficient. As a result, the end voltage is lower than that of other strings. Cause ID 1 = PV1 Cause ID 2 = PV2 Cause ID 3 = PV3 Cause ID 4 = PV4
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Check whether the number of PV modules connected in series to this PV string is less than the number of PV modules connected in series to the other PV strings connected in parallel with this PV string. If yes, wait until the PV string current decreases below 0.5 A, set DC SWITCH to OFF, and adjust the number of PV modules in the PV string. 2. Check whether the PV string is shaded. 3. Check whether the open-circuit voltage of the PV string is normal.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: AFCI Check Failure / AFCI self- check failure / AFCI Self-Check Failure
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: AFCI Check Failure / AFCI self- check failure / AFCI Self-Check Failure. The AFCI check fails. Cause ID = 1, 2 ● 1: The AFCI check circuit is abnormal. ● 2: The AFCI circuit is faulty.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The AFCI check fails. Cause ID = 1, 2 ● 1: The AFCI check circuit is abnormal. ● 2: The AFCI circuit is faulty.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Turn off the AC output switch and DC input switch, and then turn them on after 5 minutes. If the fault persists, contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Phase wire short- circuited to PE / Phase Wire Short-Circuited to PE
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Phase wire short- circuited to PE / Phase Wire Short-Circuited to PE. Cause ID = 1 The impedance of the output phase wire to PE is low or the output phase wire is short- circuited to PE.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The impedance of the output phase wire to PE is low or the output phase wire is short- circuited to PE.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check the impedance of the output phase wire to PE, locate the position with lower impedance, and rectify the fault.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Grid Failure / Grid loss / Grid Loss
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid Failure / Grid loss / Grid Loss. Cause ID = 1 ● Power grid outage occurs. ● The AC circuit is disconnected or the AC switch is off.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 ● Power grid outage occurs. ● The AC circuit is disconnected or the AC switch is off.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. The alarm is cleared automatically after the power grid recovers. 2. Check whether the AC circuit is disconnected or the AC switch is off.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Grid Undervolt age / Grid Undervoltage / Grid undervolt age
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid Undervolt age / Grid Undervoltage / Grid undervolt age. Cause ID = 1 The grid voltage is below the lower threshold or the low-voltage duration has lasted for more than the value specified by low voltage ride-through (LVRT).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The grid voltage is below the lower threshold or the low-voltage duration has lasted for more than the value specified by low voltage ride-through (LVRT).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. If the alarm occurs occasionally, the power grid may be abnormal temporarily. The SUN2000 automatically recovers after detecting that the power grid becomes normal. 2. If the alarm persists, check whether the power grid voltage is within the acceptable range. If not, contact the local power operator. If yes, modify the grid undervoltage protection threshold through the mobile app, SmartLogger, or network management system (NMS) with the consent of the local power operator. 3. If the alarm persists for a long time, check the connection between the AC circuit breaker and the output power cable.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Grid Overvolta ge / Grid Overvoltage / Grid overvolta ge
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid Overvolta ge / Grid Overvoltage / Grid overvolta ge. Cause ID = 1 The grid voltage exceeds the higher threshold or the high voltage duration has lasted for more than the value specified by HVRT.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The grid voltage exceeds the higher threshold or the high voltage duration has lasted for more than the value specified by HVRT.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. If the alarm occurs accidentally, the power grid may be abnormal temporarily. The inverter automatically recovers after detecting that the power grid becomes normal. 2. If the alarm occurs frequently, check whether the power grid voltage is within the acceptable range. If no, contact the local power operator. If yes, log in to the mobile app, SmartLogger, or network management system (NMS) to modify the grid overvoltage protection threshold with the consent of the local power operator. 3. Check whether the peak voltage of the power grid is too high. If the fault persists and cannot be rectified for a long time, contact the power grid operator.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Unbalance d Grid Voltage / Grid volt. Imbalanc e / Grid Voltage Imbalance. Cause ID = 1 The difference between grid phase voltages exceeds the upper threshold.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The difference between grid phase voltages exceeds the upper threshold.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. If the alarm occurs accidentally, the power grid may be abnormal temporarily. The inverter automatically recovers after detecting that the power grid becomes normal. 2. If the alarm occurs frequently, check whether the power grid voltage is within the acceptable range. If no, contact the local power operator. 3. If the fault persists for a long time, check the connection of the AC output power cables. 4. If the AC output power cables are correctly connected, but the alarm persists and affects the energy yield of the PV plant, contact the local power operator.
