vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Abnormal Grounding / Abnormal groundin g. Cause ID = 1 ● The neutral wire or PE cable of the inverter is not connected. ● The output mode set for the inverter is inconsistent with the cable connection mode.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 ● The neutral wire or PE cable of the inverter is not connected. ● The output mode set for the inverter is inconsistent with the cable connection mode.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Power off the inverter (turn off the AC output switch and DC input switch, and wait for a period of time. For details about the wait time, see the description on the device safety warning label), and then perform the following operations: 1. Check whether the PE cable for the inverter is connected properly. 2. If the inverter is connected to a TN power grid, check whether the neutral wire is properly connected and whether the voltage of the neutral wire to ground is normal. 3. After the inverter is powered on, check whether the output mode set for the inverter is consistent with the output cable connection mode.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Low Insulation Resistance / Low insulation resistance
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Low Insulation Resistance / Low insulation resistance. Cause ID = 1 ● A short circuit occurs between the PV array and the ground. ● The ambient air of the PV array is damp and the insulation between the PV array and the ground is poor.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 ● A short circuit occurs between the PV array and the ground. ● The ambient air of the PV array is damp and the insulation between the PV array and the ground is poor.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Check the output impedance of the PV array to ground. If there is a short circuit or lack of insulation, rectify it. 2. Check that the PE cable of the SUN2000 is correctly connected. 3. If the impedance is lower than the specified protection threshold in rainy and cloudy days, set Insulation resistance protection using the mobile app, SmartLogger, or NMS. Current insulation resistance: x MΩ, possible short circuit position: x%. The short circuit position is valid for a single PV string. If there are multiple PV strings, check the PV strings one by one. For details, see F Locating Insulation Resistance Faults. For details about how to query the low insulation resistance alarm, see HUAWEI SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 and SUN6000-5/6KTL- L0 Inverter Low Insulation Resistance Fault Indication Guide.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Faulty Power Collector. Cause ID = 1 The smart power sensor is disconnected.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The smart power sensor is disconnected.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Check that the configured power meter model is the same as the actual model. 2. Check that the communications parameters for the smart power sensors are the same as the SUN2000 RS485 configurations. 3. Check whether the smart power sensor is powered on and whether the RS485 communications cable is connected.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery Abnormal / Battery abnormal. The battery is faulty, disconnected, or the battery circuit breaker is OFF when the battery is running. Cause ID = 1–4 ● 1: The battery communication is abnormal. ● 2: The battery port experiences overcurrent. ● 3. The battery enabling cable is not properly connected. ● 4: The battery port voltage is abnormal.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The battery is faulty, disconnected, or the battery circuit breaker is OFF when the battery is running. Cause ID = 1–4 ● 1: The battery communication is abnormal. ● 2: The battery port experiences overcurrent. ● 3. The battery enabling cable is not properly connected. ● 4: The battery port voltage is abnormal.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. If the battery fault indicator is steady on or blinking, contact the battery supplier. 2. Check that the battery enabling/power/ communications cable is correctly installed, and that the communications parameters are the same as the SUN2000 RS485 configurations. 3. Check that the auxiliary power switch on the battery is set to ON. 4. Send a shutdown command on the app. Turn off the AC output switch, DC input switch, and battery switch. Then turn on the battery switch, AC output switch, and DC input switch in sequence after 5 minutes. 5. If the fault persists, contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Active Islanding. Cause ID = 1 When the power grid experiences an AC power outage, the SUN2000 detects islanding proactively.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 When the power grid experiences an AC power outage, the SUN2000 detects islanding proactively.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check that the grid connection voltage of the SUN2000 is normal.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Transient AC overvolta ge / Transient AC Overvoltage
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Transient AC overvolta ge / Transient AC Overvoltage. Cause ID = 1 The inverter detects that the phase voltage exceeds the transient AC overvoltage protection threshold.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The inverter detects that the phase voltage exceeds the transient AC overvoltage protection threshold.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Check whether the grid connection voltage exceeds the upper threshold. If yes, contact the local power operator. 2. If you have confirmed that the grid connection voltage exceeds the upper threshold and obtained the consent of the local power operator, modify the overvoltage protection threshold. 3. Check that the peak grid voltage does not exceed the upper threshold.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Peripheral Port Short Circuit. Cause ID = 1 The SUN2000L Alarm port is short-circuited when supplying 12 V power to peripheral.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The SUN2000L Alarm port is short-circuited when supplying 12 V power to peripheral.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Turn off the AC and DC switches on the SUN2000L and disconnect the peripheral from the Alarm port. Then power on the SUN2000L again and clear the alarm manually. If the alarm persists, contact your supplier to rectify the fault. If the alarm is cleared, it indicates that the port on the SUN2000L is normal. Then check that the pins in the power port on the peripheral are not short-circuited. 2. Replace the peripheral, and then start a self-check to check that the port functions properly.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Off-grid output overload. Cause ID = 1/2 The output is overloaded or short-circuited.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1/2 The output is overloaded or short-circuited.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Check whether the device output is short- circuited. 2. Check whether the device load configuration exceeds the rated value.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Abnormal PV Module Configuration / Abnormal PV module configuration
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Abnormal PV Module Configuration / Abnormal PV module configuration. ● Cause ID = 1 The number of optimizers connected to the inverter exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 2 The PV string power or the number of optimizers connected in series in a PV string exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 3 The number of optimizers connected in series in a PV string is less than the lower threshold, the PV string output is reversely connected, or the output of some optimizers in the PV string is reversely connected. ● Cause ID = 4 The number of PV strings connected to the inverter exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 5 The PV string output is reversely connected or the PV string output is short-circuited. ● Cause ID = 6 Under the same MPPT, the number of optimizers connected in series in PV strings connected in parallel is different, or the output of some optimizers in PV strings is reversely connected. ● Cause ID = 7 The optimizer installation position is changed, or PV strings are combined or exchanged. ● Cause ID = 8 The sunlight is weak or changes abnormally. ● Cause ID = 9 In partial configuration scenarios, the PV string voltage exceeds the inverter input voltage specifications.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: ● Cause ID = 1 The number of optimizers connected to the inverter exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 2 The PV string power or the number of optimizers connected in series in a PV string exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 3 The number of optimizers connected in series in a PV string is less than the lower threshold, the PV string output is reversely connected, or the output of some optimizers in the PV string is reversely connected. ● Cause ID = 4 The number of PV strings connected to the inverter exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID = 5 The PV string output is reversely connected or the PV string output is short-circuited. ● Cause ID = 6 Under the same MPPT, the number of optimizers connected in series in PV strings connected in parallel is different, or the output of some optimizers in PV strings is reversely connected. ● Cause ID = 7 The optimizer installation position is changed, or PV strings are combined or exchanged. ● Cause ID = 8 The sunlight is weak or changes abnormally. ● Cause ID = 9 In partial configuration scenarios, the PV string voltage exceeds the inverter input voltage specifications.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check whether the total number of PV modules, number of PV modules in a PV string, and number of PV strings meet requirements and whether the PV module output is reversely connected. ● Cause ID 1: Check whether the total number of optimizers exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID 2: Check whether the PV string power or the number of PV strings connected in series exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID 3: 1. Check whether the number of optimizers connected in series in the PV string is below the lower threshold. 2. Check whether the PV string output is reversely connected. 3. Check whether the PV string output is disconnected. 4. Check whether the optimizer output extension cable is correct (positive connector at one end and negative connector at the other). ● Cause ID 4: Check whether the number of PV strings exceeds the upper threshold. ● Cause ID 5: Check whether the PV string output is reversely connected or short- circuited. ● Cause ID 6: 1. Check whether the number of optimizers connected in series in the PV strings connected in parallel under the same MPPT is the same. 2. Check whether the optimizer output extension cable is correct (positive connector at one end and negative connector at the other). ● Cause ID 7: When the sunlight is normal, perform the optimizer search function again. ● Cause ID 8: When the sunlight is normal, perform the optimizer search function again. ● Cause ID 9: Calculate the PV string voltage based on the number of PV modules in the string and check whether the string voltage exceeds the upper threshold of the inverter input voltage.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Optimizer Fault / Optimize r fault. Cause ID = 1 An optimizer is faulty.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 An optimizer is faulty.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Go to the optimizer information page to view the fault information.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Grid-tied/Off- grid Controller Abnormal / Grid-tied/ Off-grid Controlle r Abnormal
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid-tied/Off- grid Controller Abnormal / Grid-tied/ Off-grid Controlle r Abnormal. Cause ID = 1 The inverter fails to communicate with the Smart Backup Box. Cause ID = 2 An unrecoverable fault occurs on a circuit inside the Smart Backup Box.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 The inverter fails to communicate with the Smart Backup Box. Cause ID = 2 An unrecoverable fault occurs on a circuit inside the Smart Backup Box.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Send a shutdown command on the app. Turn off the AC output switch, DC input switch, and battery switch. 2. Check whether the power cable and RS485 cable between the Smart Backup Box and the inverter are normal. 3. After 5 minutes, turn on the battery switch, AC output side, AC output switch, and DC input switch. 4. If the alarm persists, contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Power yield is lower than expected based on current light.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Power yield is lower than expected based on current light.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Clean the PV modules (dirt/bird droppings).
2. Check for new shading (trees/antennas).
3. Use "IV Curve Diagnosis" in the app to check for micro-cracks or cell degradation.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: RS485 communication cable is disconnected, loose, or interference is too high.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: RS485 communication cable is disconnected, loose, or interference is too high.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check the RJ45 or terminal connections at the inverter COM port and battery control unit.
2. Verify that Pins 1/2 or 7/9 are correctly crimped (Huawei uses specific pins, not standard T568B).
3. Ensure the communication cable is shielded and grounded at one end.
4. Restart the communication dongle/SmartLogger to refresh the Modbus loop.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal software logic error or conflicting commands from the SmartLogger.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal software logic error or conflicting commands from the SmartLogger.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Perform a firmware update for both the Inverter and the Battery Control Module.
2. Check if there are third-party EMS systems sending conflicting Modbus commands.
3. Restart the battery DC switch.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The battery has reached 0% SOC and remained there too long, entering "hibernation."
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The battery has reached 0% SOC and remained there too long, entering "hibernation."
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Immediate: Press the "Black Start" button on the side of the battery for 5 seconds.
2. Enable "Emergency Charge from Grid" in the FusionSolar app settings.
3. Ensure the battery DC switch is ON. If it won't stay ON, the voltage is too low for the internal relay to hold.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal hardware failure in one of the 5kWh/7kWh power modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal hardware failure in one of the 5kWh/7kWh power modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Observe the LED rings on each battery module; a steady red ring identifies the faulty pack.
2. Turn off the DC switch, wait 5 minutes, and restart.
3. If the error persists, isolate the faulty module (disconnect its DC/Comm links) to allow the rest of the stack to operate.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Discharge power exceeds the maximum rating of the battery modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Discharge power exceeds the maximum rating of the battery modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check if high-power appliances (motors, pumps) started simultaneously while off-grid.
2. Add more battery modules to the stack to increase the maximum discharge power (P_{max}).
3. Review your "Backup Power" settings to limit discharge during outages.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Charging voltage exceeds safety limits (usually a BMS calibration issue).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Charging voltage exceeds safety limits (usually a BMS calibration issue).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. The system usually recovers automatically. If not, perform a "Battery SOC Calibration" in the app maintenance menu.
2. Check for firmware updates that address BMS charging logic.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Sudden spike in current during charging or discharging.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Sudden spike in current during charging or discharging.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check for short circuits in the DC cables between the inverter and battery.
2. The system will attempt to auto-recover 3 times. If it fails, a manual restart is required.
3. Inspect the DC fuse inside the Battery Control Unit (BCU).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: High ambient temperature; blocked air vents; direct sunlight.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: High ambient temperature; blocked air vents; direct sunlight.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ensure at least 15cm of clearance around all sides of the battery modules.
2. Clean dust from the vents of the Control Unit and modules.
3. Install a sunshield if the battery is outdoors and exposed to direct sun.
4. Check if the internal cooling fan is blocked or faulty.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Temperature dropped below -10^{°}C (for S0) or -20^{°}C (for S1).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Temperature dropped below -10^{°}C (for S0) or -20^{°}C (for S1).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. In cold climates, ensure the internal "Heating Film" is enabled in settings (for S1 models).
2. If the battery is in an uninsulated garage, consider adding basic insulation or a heat source.
3. The battery will stop discharging/charging to protect the cells until it warms up.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: A major safety fault has occurred, and the BMS has locked the pack for safety.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: A major safety fault has occurred, and the BMS has locked the pack for safety.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. This usually follows a severe Overcurrent or Overvoltage event.
2. Check the "Cause ID" for the specific safety trigger.
3. Requires an installer to "Unlock" the device via the local commissioning tool after verifying wiring.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal communication failure between the Control Unit (BCU) and the modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal communication failure between the Control Unit (BCU) and the modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check the internal "Link" cables between the stacked battery modules.
2. Ensure the modules are physically seated correctly and the side locking bolts are tight.
3. Inspect the pins on the module-to-module connectors for bends or corrosion.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Detected during off-grid/backup mode; grid signal is bleeding through.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Detected during off-grid/backup mode; grid signal is bleeding through.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check the "Backup Box" or "SmartGuard" wiring. Ensure the Neutral-Earth bond is correct for your region.
2. Verify the Main Switch is fully open during a grid failure to prevent "back-feeding."

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The physical DC switch on the side of the battery is turned OFF.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The physical DC switch on the side of the battery is turned OFF.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Manually flip the switch to ON.
2. If it immediately flips back to OFF, there is a short circuit or a "Critical Fault" (see 3107) preventing startup.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_name: Monitoring Unit Faulty / Faulty monitorin g unit / Faulty Monitoring Unit
vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Monitoring Unit Faulty / Faulty monitorin g unit / Faulty Monitoring Unit. Cause ID = 1 ● The flash memory is insufficient. ● The flash memory has bad sectors.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cause ID = 1 ● The flash memory is insufficient. ● The flash memory has bad sectors.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Turn off the AC output switch and DC input switch, and then turn them on after 5 minutes. If the alarm persists, replace the monitoring board or contact your dealer or Huawei technical support.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SUN2000-2-6KTL-L1 / Single-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-5-10KTL-M1 / Three-phase Residential Inverter; SUN2000-8-20KTL-M2 / Three-phase String Inverter; SUN2000L-(2KTL-5KTL) / Single-phase Residential Inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Metrology chip failure or voltage spike damage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Metrology chip failure or voltage spike damage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Restart the meter by flipping its breaker.
2. If "Err" persists, the meter hardware is damaged and needs replacement.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Minor communication lag or environmental warning.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Minor communication lag or environmental warning.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check for "String Abnormal" or "Grid Frequency" warnings.
2. Often clears itself; if it persists, check the RS485 cabling for noise.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Critical device offline (Inverter or Battery) or internal fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Critical device offline (Inverter or Battery) or internal fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Log into the SmartLogger WebUI (Enspire).
2. Navigate to "Active Alarms" to see which specific device is failing.
3. Check the "Maintenance" tab for hardware self-test results.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The optimizer is not communicating with the inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The optimizer is not communicating with the inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Search: Run "Optimizer Search" from the Inverter Maintenance menu.
2. Sunlight: Ensure there is enough irradiance (>50 W/m^2) to "wake up" the optimizer.
3. Distance: Check if the string length or cable distance exceeds the PLC communication limit.
4. SN Check: Verify the Serial Number (SN) in the physical layout matches the actual device.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Panel V_{oc} is higher than the optimizer's limit (e.g., >80V for 600W models).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Panel V_{oc} is higher than the optimizer's limit (e.g., >80V for 600W models).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Compare the panel datasheet (V_{oc} at min. temp) with the optimizer’s maximum input voltage.
2. If you are using "2-in-1" wiring (two panels to one 1100W optimizer), ensure they are in parallel, not series, if voltage is the issue.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Current Transformers (CTs) installed backwards.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Current Transformers (CTs) installed backwards.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check the arrow on the CTs; it must point toward the load (house).
2. Ensure Phase L1 CT is on Phase L1 wire (mixing phases causes wrong data).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: RS485 loop is open or addresses are duplicated.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: RS485 loop is open or addresses are duplicated.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ensure every inverter in the chain has a unique RS485 address (1, 2, 3...).
2. Check the 120\Omega termination resistor switch at the end of the long RS485 run.
3. Measure voltage on the RS485 line (should be approx. 2-5V DC).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Blown fuse or incorrect wiring to terminals 1/2/3.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Blown fuse or incorrect wiring to terminals 1/2/3.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Use a multimeter to check for voltage at the meter's input terminals.
2. Ensure the protection fuses for the meter haven't tripped.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Current is flowing backward into the optimizer (common in parallel strings).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Current is flowing backward into the optimizer (common in parallel strings).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check for severe shading on one string vs. another on the same MPPT.
2. Inspect the string for mismatched panel types or counts.
3. Verify all strings on the same MPPT have the same number of optimizers.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Ambient heat is too high or airflow behind the panel is blocked.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Ambient heat is too high or airflow behind the panel is blocked.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ensure the optimizer is mounted to the racking rail, not directly against the hot panel backsheet.
2. Check for bird nests or debris blocking airflow behind the modules.
3. The device will auto-resume once it cools below 70°\text{C}.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No SIM card (4G) or no Router connection (WLAN).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No SIM card (4G) or no Router connection (WLAN).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. 4G: Verify the SIM card is inserted correctly and has an active data plan.
2. WLAN: Ensure the router is ON and within range (no more than 10-15m).
3. Re-run the "Quick Settings" wizard in the App.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Connected to router but can't reach Huawei Cloud.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Connected to router but can't reach Huawei Cloud.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check if the router has internet access.
2. Ensure Firewall ports 27250 and 16100 are open.
3. Check if the DNS is set to a reliable source (like 8.8.8.8).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal hardware failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal hardware failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Remove the Dongle and re-insert it firmly.
2. Check if the USB port on the inverter is clean/dry.
3. If it stays red on multiple inverters, the Dongle needs replacement.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal hardware failure or critical safety trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal hardware failure or critical safety trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Reset: Power cycle the DC and AC of the inverter and wait 5 minutes.
2. Isolate: If the red icon persists, swap the "faulty" optimizer with a known working one nearby to see if the fault follows the device or the panel.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Dongle not talking to the Inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Dongle not talking to the Inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ensure the Inverter's RS485 settings match the Dongle (standard is Baud 9600).
2. Check if the Dongle is compatible with that specific inverter model.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Communication was interrupted during a software update.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Communication was interrupted during a software update.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ensure sunlight is stable (don't update at dusk).
2. Move the mobile device closer to the Smart Dongle/Logger during the update.
3. Retry the update; the optimizer will keep its old firmware until a "Success" is reached.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The optimizer was seen before but is now missing from the string loop.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The optimizer was seen before but is now missing from the string loop.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Physical Link: Check for a loose MC4 connector or a cut cable in the string.
2. Bypass: Check if the optimizer has entered "Bypass Mode" due to a severe panel mismatch.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: MCU or Control Logic fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: MCU or Control Logic fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Hard reboot (AC/DC off).
2. Verify firmware version.
3. Check internal logs for specific sub-codes.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Memory corruption or R/W failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Memory corruption or R/W failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Factory reset.
2. Reflash firmware.
3. Reconfigure parameters manually.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Relay stuck (open or closed).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Relay stuck (open or closed).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Listen for the physical "click" during startup.
2. Check AC grid stability/voltage.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Hall effect sensor failure or offset.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Hall effect sensor failure or offset.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Inspect sensor wiring harness.
2. Compare internal readings vs. external clamp meter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: General PCB/Internal failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: General PCB/Internal failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Visual inspection for "magic smoke" or bulging caps.
2. Check for thermal hotspots on the board.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Power module/Gate driver failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Power module/Gate driver failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Caution: Check DC insulation before restarting.
2. Inspect cooling/thermal paste integrity.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Cooling obstruction or motor failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cooling obstruction or motor failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Manual rotation check (debris).
2. Test 12V/24V power supply to the fan header.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Physical relay damage (pitted contacts).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Physical relay damage (pitted contacts).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Continuity test across poles (when de-energized).
2. Replace relay or power board.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Sensor drift or reference voltage loss.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Sensor drift or reference voltage loss.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Attempt manual recalibration via service software.
2. Verify internal DC bus voltage accuracy.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Digital Signal Processor sync issue.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Digital Signal Processor sync issue.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Firmware update.
2. Check for EMI/interference from nearby high-power lines.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Local grid high or "Voltage Rise" in AC cables.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Local grid high or "Voltage Rise" in AC cables.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Measure AC voltage at inverter terminals.
2. Check AC cable gauge: Undersized wires cause voltage rise.
3. Verify Grid Standard (e.g., IEEE 1547).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid sag or loose AC connections.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid sag or loose AC connections.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check for loose terminals or tripped AC breakers.
2. Inspect transformer tap settings if site-wide.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid instability or Generator "drift."
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid instability or Generator "drift."
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. If using a backup generator, check the governor/Hz.
2. Monitor for utility-side frequency fluctuations.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Total loss of AC sync.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Total loss of AC sync.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Confirm AC breaker is ON.
2. Check for "Island Mode" or blackout in the area.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: 10-minute average voltage drift.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: 10-minute average voltage drift.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Monitor voltage over a 24hr cycle.
2. Contact utility if average voltage consistently exceeds limits.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Missing L1, L2, or L3.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Missing L1, L2, or L3.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Measure phase-to-phase and phase-to-neutral.
2. Check for blown fuses in the AC disconnect.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Asymmetrical phase voltages.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Asymmetrical phase voltages.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check for heavy single-phase loads on the site.
2. Verify all AC terminal torque settings.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: External fault or "Short" to ground.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: External fault or "Short" to ground.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. STOP: Disconnect AC and Megger the AC cables.
2. Check for "nicked" insulation or water in the conduit.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Protection trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Protection trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Verify grid stability.
2. Check if multiple inverters are "fighting" on one bus.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Incorrect regional settings.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Incorrect regional settings.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Access the Safety Parameter menu.
2. Select the correct Country/Grid code for the region.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: String V_{oc} exceeds inverter Max Input.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: String V_{oc} exceeds inverter Max Input.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Danger: Disconnect DC immediately.
2. Measure V_{oc} of the string.
3. Recalculate for cold weather expansion.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: String voltage below Startup/MPPT range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: String voltage below Startup/MPPT range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check for undersized strings (too few panels).
2. Verify all MC4 connectors are fully seated.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Leakage current to ground (often moisture).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Leakage current to ground (often moisture).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Perform an Insulation Resistance Test (Megger).
2. Inspect for "nicked" cables or water in MC4s.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC Positive/Negative swapped.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: DC Positive/Negative swapped.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Use a multimeter to verify +/- at the inverter input.
2. Correct the wiring at the string level.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Open circuit or disconnected DC.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Open circuit or disconnected DC.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Verify DC Isolator/Switch is ON.
2. Check for blown string fuses (if applicable).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Low irradiance or extreme shading.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Low irradiance or extreme shading.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check for heavy snow, dust, or "hard shading" (trees).
2. Verify if the inverter is stuck in "Waiting" mode.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Imbalance between parallel strings.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Imbalance between parallel strings.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ensure all strings on the same MPPT have the same panel count.
2. Check for mismatched panel models.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Direct short between DC and Earth.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Direct short between DC and Earth.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Inspect the racking and grounding lugs.
2. Check if a cable is pinched under a module frame.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal sensor drift or Hall fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal sensor drift or Hall fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Power cycle the inverter.
2. If it persists without load, the internal sensor is likely faulty.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal DC Bus spike.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal DC Bus spike.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Restart inverter immediately.
2. Compare PV input voltage with the Max DC Bus rating.
3. Check for external grid surges pushing back.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal Bus cannot maintain charge.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal Bus cannot maintain charge.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Verify PV input is sufficient for the "Startup" threshold.
2. Check for failing internal boost converters.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Split-capacitor voltage mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Split-capacitor voltage mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Power cycle to re-balance.
2. Visual Inspection: Look for "bulging" or leaking electrolytic capacitors.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Pre-charge circuit failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Pre-charge circuit failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ensure DC input is stable during the 60s countdown.
2. If it fails repeatedly at 0 seconds, the soft-start resistor is likely blown.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Sudden internal/external demand.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Sudden internal/external demand.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Inspect AC and DC wiring for intermittent shorts.
2. Restart to clear "phantom" current spikes.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Critical output/IGBT short.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Critical output/IGBT short.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. STOP: Disconnect AC load and PV strings.
2. Inspect AC terminals for carbon tracking (arc marks).
3. Replace inverter if the fault clears only when loads are removed.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Ribbon cable or internal bus fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Ribbon cable or internal bus fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Restart inverter.
2. If persistent, internal board-to-board cables may be loose.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Hardware or sync failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Hardware or sync failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Reseat the WiFi dongle/module.
2. Check for "Steady Blue/Green" LED on the module.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DNS or Firewall blockage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: DNS or Firewall blockage.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ping the inverter's IP from a local laptop.
2. Check if the router is blocking Ports 80, 443, or 502.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Wiring polarity or termination.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Wiring polarity or termination.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Swap A/B wires if using a 3rd party logger.
2. Check for 120Ω termination resistor at the end of the chain.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: BMS handshake failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: BMS handshake failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Battery comm cable: Ensure it is a "Straight-through" or "Crossover" as required.
2. Verify Battery ID/Address settings.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: External data logger power.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: External data logger power.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Verify the logger is receiving 5V/12V DC power.
2. Check for firmware compatibility between logger and inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Flash memory "Read/Write" error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Flash memory "Read/Write" error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Attempt a System Reset to clear the cache.
2. Reflash firmware to re-partition the memory.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Charge limit exceeded.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Charge limit exceeded.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Verify "Max Charge Voltage" in inverter settings matches the battery datasheet.
2. Check for BMS "Cell Imbalance" causing a total pack spike.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Voltage below "Cut-off."
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Voltage below "Cut-off."
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. If the battery is "asleep," use a Force Charge or "Jumpstart" if supported.
2. Increase the "Minimum SOC" (State of Charge) to 15-20% to prevent deep discharge.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Handshake failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Handshake failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check the RJ45/CAN cable pinout (T-568B vs. Custom).
2. Verify the Battery ID/Dip-switch settings on the master battery.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Thermal limit reached.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Thermal limit reached.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. High Temp: Improve ventilation/cooling in the battery room.
2. Low Temp: Ensure batteries are not on a cold concrete floor; use a thermal pad or heater.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Logic or Relay failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Logic or Relay failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check if the "Time of Use" or "Peak Shaving" settings are conflicting with the actual load.
2. Inspect DC breakers/fuses between the battery and inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal Cell/BMS failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal Cell/BMS failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Power down the entire system and measure the static voltage of each battery module.
2. Contact the battery manufacturer if one module is significantly lower than others.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC current leaking into AC side.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: DC current leaking into AC side.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Restart inverter.
2. Check for high-frequency interference or a weak grid neutral.
3. Verify the AC grounding point is solid.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Residual/Leakage current to Earth.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Residual/Leakage current to Earth.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Caution: Possible shock hazard. Do not touch racking.
2. Perform an Insulation Resistance Test on both AC and DC runs.
3. Look for "weeping" conduits or water in junction boxes.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: AC Surge Protector "spent."
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: AC Surge Protector "spent."
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Visual Check: Look for a "Red" indicator on the AC surge modules.
2. Replace the plug-in SPD module if the internal fuse has blown.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC Surge Protector "spent."
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: DC Surge Protector "spent."
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check for recent lightning or grid surges.
2. Replace the DC SPD module. Note: The inverter may still run, but it is unprotected.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: General Safety Logic trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: General Safety Logic trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check for concurrent "IE" codes to find the root cause.
2. Ensure all external emergency stop (E-Stop) buttons are released.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Corrupt image or "Incomplete" update.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Corrupt image or "Incomplete" update.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Re-flash the latest stable firmware version.
2. Clear internal cache via Factory Reset if supported.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Illegal value or "Out of Range" setting.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Illegal value or "Out of Range" setting.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Audit the Safety Standard and Battery Voltage settings.
2. Check if a newly added parameter is incompatible with the older hardware version.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Startup self-check timeout.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Startup self-check timeout.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Perform a "Hard Reboot" (AC/DC off for 5 min).
2. Check for stable PV and Grid voltage during the 60s countdown.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: CT Clamp or Smart Meter mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: CT Clamp or Smart Meter mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check CT orientation: Ensure the arrow points toward the Grid (not the Load).
2. Verify RS485 wiring (A/B) to the meter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Demand Response Mode (DRM0) trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Demand Response Mode (DRM0) trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check the DRM port jumper (often required in Australia/NZ).
2. Verify if the utility is remotely limiting power via the DRM interface.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Dry contact or Cloud command.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Dry contact or Cloud command.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check the "Remote Shutdown" terminal for a closed/open circuit.
2. Check the App/Portal for an "Active Stop" command.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Relay or Logic check failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Relay or Logic check failure.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Ensure the inverter is disconnected from the Grid during the test.
2. If it fails on "Step 3/4," an internal relay is likely stuck.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Load mismatch or "Export Limit" hit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Load mismatch or "Export Limit" hit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check the Export Power Limit in the settings.
2. Verify if the AC load exceeds the inverter's surge capacity (P_{max}).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Undefined firmware state.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Undefined firmware state.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Update firmware to see if the code resolves to a known "IE" code.
2. Contact Tech Support with the Serial Number and Internal Logs.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: AC HCT sensor/current measurement fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an AC HCT/current measurement fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: SPI communication/internal interface fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an SPI communication/internal interface fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: SCI communication/internal interface fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an SCI communication/internal interface fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Inverter EEPROM fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an inverter EEPROM fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Relay fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates a relay fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Sampling error listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates a sampling/measurement error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare integrità delle stringhe fotovoltaiche e riavviare l'inverter. / Check PV string integrity and restart the inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Residual current device/detection fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Possible insulation/leakage path between DC/AC circuits and earth.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare l'impedenza tra ciascun ingresso CC e la vite di terra e tra l'uscita CA e la vite di terra. / Check impedance between each DC input and earth screw and between AC output and earth screw.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: PV configuration error listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PV mode configured on display may be incorrect.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare la modalità fotovoltaica inserita a display. / Check the photovoltaic mode entered on the display.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid lost / no grid condition listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid voltage may be absent at the inverter AC connector.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare la tensione di rete al connettore CA dell'inverter. / Check grid voltage at the inverter AC connector.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: 10-minute AC voltage fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Supply voltage or AC line voltage drop may be outside expected range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare tensione di fornitura e caduta di tensione linea alternata. / Check supply voltage and AC line voltage drop.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Voltage lost condition listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Supply voltage or AC line voltage drop may be outside expected range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare tensione di fornitura e caduta di tensione linea alternata. / Check supply voltage and AC line voltage drop.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Frequency lost condition listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid voltage/frequency synchronization may be unavailable or unstable.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare tensione di rete. / Check grid voltage.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: PLL loss / grid synchronization loss listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid voltage/frequency synchronization may be unavailable or unstable.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare tensione di rete. / Check grid voltage.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC bus voltage fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PV string voltage/current may be outside inverter nameplate limits.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare che tensioni e correnti delle stringhe fotovoltaiche siano conformi ai dati di targa dell'inverter. / Check PV string voltages and currents against inverter nameplate data.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Inverter over-current protection fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PV string voltage/current may be outside inverter nameplate limits.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare che tensioni e correnti delle stringhe fotovoltaiche siano conformi ai dati di targa dell'inverter. / Check PV string voltages and currents against inverter nameplate data.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC injection over-current protection fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PV string voltage/current may be outside inverter nameplate limits.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare che tensioni e correnti delle stringhe fotovoltaiche siano conformi ai dati di targa dell'inverter. / Check PV string voltages and currents against inverter nameplate data.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: PV voltage fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PV string voltage/current may be outside inverter nameplate limits.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare che tensioni e correnti delle stringhe fotovoltaiche siano conformi ai dati di targa dell'inverter. / Check PV string voltages and currents against inverter nameplate data.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: RC fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an RC-related protection/fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Protezione TZ: Verificare l’integrità dei cavi e delle connessioni. / Check cable and connection integrity.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Software over-current protection fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates software over-current protection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Protezione TZ: Verificare l’integrità dei cavi e delle connessioni. / Check cable and connection integrity.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: TZ protection event listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cable or connection integrity issue may be present.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare l’integrità dei cavi e delle connessioni. / Check cable and connection integrity.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Other fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare l'integrità della linea CC e CA dell'inverter. / Check integrity of inverter DC and AC lines.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Manager EEPROM error listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an EEPROM manager error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare l'integrità della linea CC e CA dell'inverter. / Check integrity of inverter DC and AC lines.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Insulation fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Manual does not provide a separate cause. The source label indicates an insulation fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare l'integrità della linea CC e CA dell'inverter. / Check integrity of inverter DC and AC lines.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: High temperature condition listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Inverter ventilation may be insufficient.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare di aver garantito la corretta ventilazione dell'inverter. / Ensure correct inverter ventilation.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Meter error listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Meter-to-inverter communication cable may not work correctly.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare funzionamento cavo di comunicazione tra meter e inverter. / Check operation of the communication cable between meter and inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Ground fault listed by Peimar.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Ground line may be incorrectly installed, or TER detection may have been enabled accidentally.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Verificare la corretta installazione della linea di terra. Verificare che non sia stata erroneamente attivata la funzione Rilevamento TER (MENU > OPZIONI (password 2014) > RILEVAMENTO TER). / Check correct ground line installation and verify TER detection was not enabled by mistake.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid Over-Voltage. Utility voltage exceeds the inverter's safety threshold.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid Over-Voltage. Utility voltage exceeds the inverter's safety threshold.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check AC Wiring: Ensure the AC cable isn't too thin (causes voltage drop/rise).
2. Safety Code: Confirm the "Safety Country" matches your local grid standards.

vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal inverter fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: There are internal faults of inverter, turn OFF the “DC switch”, wait for 5 m...

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Potential Induced Degradation (PID) recovery module is active or faulted.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Potential Induced Degradation (PID) recovery module is active or faulted.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Night State: This function usually runs at night. If it alarms during the day, the internal PID module is malfunctioning.
2. Settings: Disable PID in the menu to see if the main inverter resumes.

vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal inverter fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: There are internal faults of inverter, turn OFF the “DC switch”, wait for 5 m...

vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PV string open-circuit voltage may be above inverter maximum input range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check whether too many PV modules are series connected in a PV string, thus t...

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: (PowerMagic Only) Coolant pressure or flow rate is below the threshold.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: (PowerMagic Only) Coolant pressure or flow rate is below the threshold.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Pump: Verify if the internal pump is receiving power.
2. Leaks: Inspect the cabinet floor for blue/green coolant residue.
3. Pressure: Ensure the expansion tank is at the correct level (usually 1.0–1.5 bar).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Isolation Fault. DC leakage detected from PV or Battery to ground.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Isolation Fault. DC leakage detected from PV or Battery to ground.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check PV Strings: Look for damaged insulation or moisture in MC4 connectors.
2. Check Battery: Ensure battery power cables aren't pinched or touching the metal casing.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The inverter has lost communication with the Lithium Battery BMS.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The inverter has lost communication with the Lithium Battery BMS.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. BMS Cable: Ensure the RJ45 cable is in the CAN/BMS port (not the Link port).
2. Pinout: Verify the cable pinout matches both SOFAR and your battery brand (often Pins 4 & 5 for CAN).
3. Battery Type: Re-select the correct battery brand in the "Battery Settings" menu.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery Over-Voltage. The battery voltage is higher than the allowed limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Battery Over-Voltage. The battery voltage is higher than the allowed limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Configuration: Check if the "Max Charge Voltage" is set correctly for your battery stack.
2. BMS Link: If communication is broken, the inverter may misread the voltage. Fix ID045 first.

2. Wake Up: Press the battery module's "Reset" or "Start" button for 5 seconds to wake it from deep sleep.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Reserved
Communication Error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Communication Error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Wiring: Check the RS485 wires (A and B).
2. Address: Ensure the meter Modbus ID is set to 001.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Factory Reset.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Factory Reset.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Triggered if the "Reset" button was held for 10s. You must reconfigure the WiFi.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Usually indicates SOC is critically low or a minor sensor mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Usually indicates SOC is critically low or a minor sensor mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Charge: Immediately provide a charge source to prevent the battery from entering "Deep Sleep."

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Inverter Link.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Inverter Link.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Reseat: Unplug and re-plug the stick.
2. Power: Ensure the inverter DC switch is ON (the stick is powered by the inverter).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The BDU lost contact with a battery module in the stack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The BDU lost contact with a battery module in the stack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Pins: Inspect the communication pins between the modules for bends or dirt.
2. Stacking: Ensure the modules are physically clicked in and bolted tightly.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC power is present, but the unit is waiting for the grid to stabilize.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: DC power is present, but the unit is waiting for the grid to stabilize.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Wireless communication to the Smart Hub or Cloud is broken.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Wireless communication to the Smart Hub or Cloud is broken.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Recoverable error (Temperature or Current).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Recoverable error (Temperature or Current).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Current: Lower the charge/discharge limit in the inverter.
2. Temp: If below 5°C, the internal heater (if equipped) will activate. Wait for it to warm up.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: AC circuit breaker is open or the grid is down. Check the AC cable connection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: AC circuit breaker is open or the grid is down. Check the AC cable connection.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The BDU cannot assign IDs to the modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The BDU cannot assign IDs to the modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Auto-Coding: Press the "Start" button for 10s. The LEDs should flash purple/blue as it assigns IDs to the stack.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The battery is in the middle of an automatic ID assignment.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The battery is in the middle of an automatic ID assignment.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Wait: Do not interrupt this process. It usually happens during the first startup of a parallel system.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Control Pilot (Communication) signal failure with the car.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Control Pilot (Communication) signal failure with the car.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Reseat: Firmly unplug and re-insert the charging gun into the car.
2. Protocol: Ensure the car is compatible with the Type 2 standard.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: IP Mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: IP Mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Default IP: Connect your PC to LAN4 and go to 192.168.4.11.
2. Credentials: Default is admin / sofar123.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid voltage is outside the operating range (e.g., <180V or >265V).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid voltage is outside the operating range (e.g., <180V or >265V).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Utility Check: Usually a grid fluctuation. The charger will auto-recover when voltage stabilizes.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Chain Interruption.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Chain Interruption.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Termination: Check if the 120Ω resistor is active at the last inverter.
2. IDs: Every inverter must have a unique Modbus ID (01, 02, etc.).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Reversed CT Clamps.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Reversed CT Clamps.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Direction: The arrow on the CT clamp must point toward the Loads/House.
2. Phase Match: Ensure CT1 is clamped to the same phase wire that powers the L1 terminal of the meter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Inverter settings.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Inverter settings.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Enable Meter: In the inverter menu, ensure "Smart Meter" is set to "Enable".

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal components or the charging plug have overheated.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal components or the charging plug have overheated.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Cool Down: Unplug and wait 10 mins. If in direct sun, consider adding a small shade.
2. Deformation: Check the plug pins for signs of melting or discoloration.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The charger's AC input is not correctly earthed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The charger's AC input is not correctly earthed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Earthing: Ensure the Ground (PE) wire is securely connected.
2. Voltage Check: Measure the voltage between Neutral and Earth; it should be near 0V.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Leakage detected in the charger or vehicle (>30mA AC or >6mA DC).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Leakage detected in the charger or vehicle (>30mA AC or >6mA DC).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Restart: Toggle the AC breaker OFF/ON and wait 3 mins.
2. Check Cable: Inspect the charging gun for moisture or dirt.
3. Car Side: If it persists, the fault might be in the car’s onboard charger.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Network Connection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Network Connection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. WiFi: Router is too far or password changed.
2. 4G: SIM card is out of credit or has no signal.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Connected to Server.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Connected to Server.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Normal. Data is being uploaded to SOFAR Cloud.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: System OK.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: System OK.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Normal. The logger's internal processor is running.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Unit is producing power and connected to the grid.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Unit is producing power and connected to the grid.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal inverter failure. Check the specific error code via the SOFAR Cloud app.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal inverter failure. Check the specific error code via the SOFAR Cloud app.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: General BMS hardware failure or critical safety trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: General BMS hardware failure or critical safety trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Hard Reset: Turn off the BDU DC switch, wait 3 minutes, then restart.
2. Check App: Look for specific sub-codes (like "Cell Unbalance").

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Insulation impedance is too low. Check the DC cables for pinches against the rack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Insulation impedance is too low. Check the DC cables for pinches against the rack.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: SPI communication fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal communication, memory, controller or firmware/device electronics fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X1-Smart Series 6–8 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.

vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: PV/DC input, insulation, bus voltage or DC current condition is outside permitted range.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Reset the PV connection. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.

vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X1-Smart Series 6–8 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Residual Current Device fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Meter, grounding, leakage current or residual current detection condition is abnormal.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Check the impedance of DC input and AC output. Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X1-Smart Series 6–8 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Over current fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Current has exceeded the permitted limit or current sensor/protection circuit detected abnormal current.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Wait for a while to check if it goes back to normal status. Disconnect PV+ and PV-, reconnect them. Or seek help from us if it cannot go back to normal state.
vendor_mappings.device_family: SolaX X1 Series 0.6–3.6 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X1-Smart Series 6–8 kW single-phase PV inverter; SolaX X3 Series 4.0–10.0 kW three-phase PV inverter

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Grid is lost
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Grid voltage/frequency/phase condition is outside permitted range or grid connection is unstable.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: System will reconnect if the utility is back to normal. Or seek help from us.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Controller fault
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal communication, memory, controller or firmware/device electronics fault.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Contact with us if this happens frequently.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: DC Injection current is too high.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: DC Injection current is too high.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Transient: Usually caused by a grid disturbance. Restart the unit.
2. Hardware: Persistent errors indicate a faulty DCI detection circuit.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal memory error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal memory error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Restart: Cycle the power. If it stays on the screen, the internal control board (DSP) needs replacement.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Communication loss between Inverter and Battery.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Communication loss between Inverter and Battery.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Cable: Ensure the CAT5/6 cable is in the BMS/CAN port, not the Meter port.
2. Pinout: SolaX G4 uses a specific pinout (typically Pins 4 & 5 for CAN).
3. Battery Type: Confirm the correct battery (e.g., T-BAT) is selected in the menu.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The Battery BMS has reported an internal safety trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The Battery BMS has reported an internal safety trip.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Battery: Look at the LEDs on the battery pack itself.
2. Restart: Turn off the battery DC switch, wait 2 minutes, then restart the battery before the inverter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: (EPS Mode) Loads exceed the inverter's backup capacity.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: (EPS Mode) Loads exceed the inverter's backup capacity.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Shed Loads: Turn off high-power items (kettles, AC).
2. Reset: Press 'ESC' on the inverter to clear the latch. If it repeats, the load is too high for the battery/inverter combo.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: (EPS Mode) Over-current detected in the backup circuit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: (EPS Mode) Over-current detected in the backup circuit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Short Circuit: Check for a short circuit in your dedicated "backup" consumer unit.
2. Non-linear Loads: Some motors have high inrush currents; try starting them one by one.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The internal changeover relay is stuck or failing.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The internal changeover relay is stuck or failing.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Power Cycle: Disconnect PV, Battery, and AC for 5 minutes.
2. Professional Help: If the error persists after a clean boot, the internal relay may need replacement.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: (Parallel Systems) Master/Slave communication error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: (Parallel Systems) Master/Slave communication error.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. CAN Link: Check the network cables between the multiple inverters.
2. Settings: Ensure only one unit is set to "Leader" and others to "Follower".

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Hardware Over-current Protection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Hardware Over-current Protection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Instant Trip: Usually caused by a sudden surge. Try a reboot.
2. PV String: Measure the PV voltage to ensure it hasn't spiked above the safety limit.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal communication failure or module mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal communication failure or module mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Cables: Ensure the internal RS485/CAN link cables between modules are clicked in.
2. Restart: Turn off the battery breaker, wait 5 minutes, then restart.
3. Firmware: Often caused by the Inverter and BMS having mismatched versions. Update both via SolaXCloud.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery voltage is higher than the charging limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Battery voltage is higher than the charging limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Self-Correction: Stop charging. Discharge the battery by running house loads.
2. Cell Balance: Leave the battery at 100% for 24 hours to allow internal balancing.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery is critically empty (Deep Discharge).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Battery is critically empty (Deep Discharge).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Force Charge: Use the "Force Charge" menu in the inverter's Advanced Settings.
2. PV Wake: If no grid is present, wait for strong sunlight to "jump-start" the BMS.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Charge Over-Current Protection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Charge Over-Current Protection.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Settings: Ensure the "Max Charge Current" in the inverter isn't set higher than the battery limit (e.g., 25A–30A).
2. Restart: If it trips instantly, there may be a power cable short.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery core temperature is out of range (< -10°C or > 55°C).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Battery core temperature is out of range (< -10°C or > 55°C).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Environment: If <0°C, charging will be restricted. If the battery has a built-in heater, it will activate automatically.
2. Ventilation: Check for 300mm clearance around the battery stack.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The BMS and Battery Modules are different models.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The BMS and Battery Modules are different models.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Check Labels: Ensure all modules in the stack are the same (e.g., all V2 or all V3). You cannot mix generations in one stack.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Leakage detected to the battery casing/earth.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Leakage detected to the battery casing/earth.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Plugs: Inspect the orange/black high-voltage plugs for moisture or carbon tracking.
2. Grounding: Ensure the battery rack is earthed to the same point as the inverter.
vendor_mappings.device_family: Triple Power Battery Systems (T-BAT H 5.8 / 3.0 / HS)

1. Reseat: Unplug and re-plug the dongle firmly.

vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Connecting to Router
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. WiFi Signal: The inverter is too far from the router.
