vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Connecting to Server
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Connecting to Server
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Internet Access: The router is connected, but there is no internet. Check your ISP.
2. DNS: Some firewalls block SolaX servers. Ensure ports 5279 and 5280 are open.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Normal Operation
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Normal Operation

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No RS485 Link.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No RS485 Link.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Wiring: Check the Twisted Pair (TP) cable between the Meter (Pins 24/25) and the Inverter.
2. Address: Meter Modbus ID must be 001.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Reversed CTs.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Reversed CTs.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Flip Clamp: The arrow on the CT clamp must point toward the House/Loads.
2. Phase Swap: Ensure the CT labeled "L1" is on the same wire as the "L1" voltage input on the meter.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Logic Mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Logic Mismatch.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Inverter Menu: Go to Settings -> Advanced -> Meter and ensure it is set to "Enable".

vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Cloud Disconnect.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal Hardware

vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Restart: Perform a full power cycle (AC/DC off for 5 mins).
2. Fatal: If it returns instantly, the internal power module (IGBT) is likely damaged.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Communication loss. 1. Check the RJ45 cable in the BMS/CAN port.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Communication loss. 1. Check the RJ45 cable in the BMS/CAN port.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
Communication loss. 1. Check the RJ45 cable in the BMS/CAN port.
2. Ensure the battery is powered ON before the inverter.
3. Verify the CAN cable pinout (Standard Solis pinout: Pin 4 = CAN_H, Pin 5 = CAN_L).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Setting mismatch. 1. Go to Advanced Settings -> Storage Energy Set -> Battery Select.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Setting mismatch. 1. Go to Advanced Settings -> Storage Energy Set -> Battery Select.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
Setting mismatch. 1. Go to Advanced Settings -> Storage Energy Set -> Battery Select.
2. Ensure the selected brand (e.g., Pylontech, BYD, SolisStorage) matches your hardware.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No Smart Meter link. 1. Check the RS485 twisted pair wiring between the meter and the inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No Smart Meter link. 1. Check the RS485 twisted pair wiring between the meter and the inverter.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
No Smart Meter link. 1. Check the RS485 twisted pair wiring between the meter and the inverter.
2. Ensure the meter (Eastron/Acrel) is set to Modbus Address 1.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Hardware not detected. 1. Check if the battery DC breaker has tripped.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Hardware not detected. 1. Check if the battery DC breaker has tripped.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
Hardware not detected. 1. Check if the battery DC breaker has tripped.
2. If using a new battery, it may be in "Sleep Mode." Press the battery "Wake" button for 3–5 seconds.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Too many appliances. 1. Turn off heavy loads (kettles, dryers) connected to the Backup port.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Too many appliances. 1. Turn off heavy loads (kettles, dryers) connected to the Backup port.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
Too many appliances. 1. Turn off heavy loads (kettles, dryers) connected to the Backup port.
2. Press ESC to clear. If it repeats, the load exceeds the inverter's peak backup rating.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Voltage too high. 1. Usually self-clears once the battery discharges slightly.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Voltage too high. 1. Usually self-clears once the battery discharges slightly.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
Voltage too high. 1. Usually self-clears once the battery discharges slightly.
2. If it persists, check if the "Max Charge Voltage" in settings is higher than the battery's limit.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Internal Battery Error. 1. The battery's own BMS has detected an issue (e.g., cell imbalance).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Internal Battery Error. 1. The battery's own BMS has detected an issue (e.g., cell imbalance).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
Internal Battery Error. 1. The battery's own BMS has detected an issue (e.g., cell imbalance).
2. Restart the battery first, then the inverter. Check battery LEDs for red "Alarm" lights.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Healthy. System is normal and communicating.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Healthy. System is normal and communicating.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: No action needed.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Standby. Battery is full and waiting for a load.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Standby. Battery is full and waiting for a load.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: Normal behavior during the day when solar is high.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Warning. Minor issue (e.g., Temp High or Low).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Warning. Minor issue (e.g., Temp High or Low).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Temp: Ensure the battery isn't in a hot garage (>45°C).
2. Auto-Recover: It will turn blue once the temp stabilizes.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Fault. Critical error (e.g., Over-current or Cell failure).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Fault. Critical error (e.g., Over-current or Cell failure).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Power Cycle: Turn off the battery DC breaker, wait 60s, then restart.
2. App Check: View the specific BMS alarm in SolisCloud.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Updating. Firmware update in progress.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Updating. Firmware update in progress.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: CRITICAL: Do not turn off power. The battery will reboot itself.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: External or Internal fan is blocked or failing.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: External or Internal fan is blocked or failing.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Fan Test: Go to Advanced Settings -> Password 0010 -> Fan Test.
2. Inspect: Check for bird nests, grass, or dust in the fan duct.
3. Manual Spin: If a fan is stiff to the touch, it needs replacement.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Lost link with the Export Power Manager.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Lost link with the Export Power Manager.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. RS485: Check the "Daisy Chain" wiring between the multiple inverters and the EPM box.
2. Termination: Ensure the last inverter in the chain has the 120Ω resistor engaged.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Fault. Reseat the dongle. If it stays red, it may need replacement.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Fault. Reseat the dongle. If it stays red, it may need replacement.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Normal. The logger is functioning correctly.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Normal. The logger is functioning correctly.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: No WiFi. Check if the router is on or if the password was changed.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: No WiFi. Check if the router is on or if the password was changed.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: EasyConnect Mode. The dongle is waiting to be configured via the app.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: EasyConnect Mode. The dongle is waiting to be configured via the app.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Connected. WiFi link is stable (Slow blink means data is transferring).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Connected. WiFi link is stable (Slow blink means data is transferring).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: LAN Data. Data is successfully moving through the Ethernet cable.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: LAN Data. Data is successfully moving through the Ethernet cable.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Lost link between Inverter and SBR/SBH battery.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Lost link between Inverter and SBR/SBH battery.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Cable Check: Ensure the RJ45 cable is in the "BAT-COM" port (not the LAN port).
2. Power Up: Batteries must be turned ON before the inverter.
3. Pinout: Sungrow uses a specific 1-to-1 pinout for its own batteries.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The inverter cannot see the Smart Meter (DTSU666).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The inverter cannot see the Smart Meter (DTSU666).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. A/B Polarity: Swap RS485 wires A and B on the meter side.
2. ID Check: Ensure the meter is set to ID 1.
3. Termination: For long runs, ensure the 120Ω resistor is engaged.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Power demand on the Backup port exceeds capacity.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Power demand on the Backup port exceeds capacity.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Shed Loads: Turn off high-surge items (pumps, vacuums).
2. Restart: The system usually tries to restart 3 times. If it fails, clear the fault manually in iSolarCloud.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Meter data doesn't match inverter output (CT issue).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Meter data doesn't match inverter output (CT issue).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. CT Direction: Ensure the arrow on CT clamps points toward the Loads.
2. Phase Match: CT1 must be on the same phase as the L1 voltage terminal.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The inverter lost its "heartbeat" with the battery stack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The inverter lost its "heartbeat" with the battery stack.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Check Pins: Sungrow uses a standard RJ45 cable, but pins 5 (CAN H) and 7 (CAN L) are critical.
2. Voltage Check: Measure the comm-line voltage; it should be between 3.3V – 5.0V.
3. Firmware: Use iSolarCloud to ensure the Inverter, WiNet-S2, and BMS are all on the same version.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: One or more cells have exceeded the safety voltage limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: One or more cells have exceeded the safety voltage limit.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Balance Time: This often happens at 99-100% SOC. Leave the battery at 100% (with some solar coming in) to allow the BMS to finish "passive balancing."
2. Hard Reset: If it trips the DC breaker, wait 10 minutes before switching it back on.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Significant voltage or temperature difference between modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Significant voltage or temperature difference between modules.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Calibration: The system may need an "SOC Calibration." (See below).
2. Module Check: Ensure all modules in the stack were manufactured around the same time (Check serial numbers for date codes).

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Battery internal temp is below 0°C.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Battery internal temp is below 0°C.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Self-Heater: The SBH series has an internal heater. It will draw power to warm itself up. Do not turn the system off during a freeze.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: Minor BMS notification (e.g., SOC jumping or high current).
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: Minor BMS notification (e.g., SOC jumping or high current).
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Monitor: Usually self-clearing. If it repeats, check the DC cable tightness at the Junction Box.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: An electric arc was detected in the DC cabling.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: An electric arc was detected in the DC cabling.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action: 1. Manual Clear: This is a "Latch" fault. After inspecting MC4 connectors for burns, you must manually clear this in iSolarCloud to restart.

vendor_mappings.vendor_error_description: The Fire Suppression System (Aerosol/Gas) has triggered.
vendor_mappings.vendor_probable_cause: The Fire Suppression System (Aerosol/Gas) has triggered.
vendor_mappings.vendor_recommended_action:
1. Safety: Evacuate and check for smoke.
2. False Alarm: If no fire, check the "Smoke/Heat Detector" wiring inside the cabinet for a short.
